Retried ASCII to_ascii_uppercase path for upper filter after recent VM/object optimizations

Expand autoresearch harness with parse/compile secondary metrics while preserving render as primary

Tried reverse-order scan in Locals::get to favor most recently inserted locals

Tried manual for-loop implementation for Locals::get instead of iterator find_map

name: Recover render performance after Value size reduction

description: Add Seq ValueRepr variant for serialized/iter-collected lists to avoid Arc<DynObject> wrapper overhead in render hot path
Add StrMap ValueRepr variant for context! maps and direct map attr/item paths to reduce Arc<DynObject> overhead while keeping 16-byte Value
Cache LoopState object Value and reuse StrMap/Seq variants to avoid repeated Arc<DynObject> allocations; recovers most render regression while keeping 16-byte Value
Fix StrMap semantic regression with minimal overhead: make dict() emit StrMap only when cloning an existing map with string keys, preserving kwargs behavior and snapshot order
Restore compatibility for Seq/StrMap in ArgType and mixed-map equality/ordering while retaining performance; tests pass and render slightly improves
Post-compaction control run on current StrMap/Seq-compatible 16-byte Value branch; observed temporary drift upward (~32.1us) likely machine noise
Speed up VM locals lookup/update by replacing iterator-closure scans with manual loops and pointer-equality fast checks for interned variable names
Confirmation run after locals pointer-equality patch (no code changes); measured ~30.2us, indicating positive noise but experiment already captured
Tried thread-local Arc<str> interning for context! keys to avoid per-render key allocations; hash/refcell overhead regressed render throughput
Tried precomputing key=="loop" once in Context::load to skip repeated loop-var checks; no gain and regressed
Tried manual for-loop implementation for Context::current_loop to avoid iterator adapter overhead; render regressed significantly
Add primitive fast paths in ArgType<Cow<str>> (u64/i64/bool) to avoid generic Value Display conversion overhead on hot filter calls (notably item|upper)
Control rerun on same primitive string-conversion fast path showed substantial noise drift upward (~30.7us); keep prior improved code but treat single-run variance cautiously
Tried small static cache for common integer->string conversions in ArgType<Cow<str>> plus bool static borrow; slightly worse than prior primitive fast-path approach
Tried callable cache in VM with BoxedFunction fast-path dispatch for filters/tests to bypass Value::call; extra classification/indirection regressed heavily
Added specialized builtin upper_filter(Value)->Value and registered it as default upper, bypassing generic Cow<String> arg marshalling and using allocation-free integer/boolean uppercase paths
Fixed strict/semi-strict undefined semantics in specialized upper_filter by adding state-based undefined checks; correctness restored but performance regressed notably
Retried upper specialization with semantics-preserving Cow->Value wrapper (upper_to_value) to cut no-op string allocations; still slower than ArgType primitive fast-path baseline

Reverted specialized upper_filter fast path from previous run because it violated strict/semi-strict undefined semantics; restored correct built-in upper behavior while retaining major ArgType speedups
Tried single-pass ASCII/lowercase detection in upper filter to replace is_ascii()+any double scan; regressed on workload
Tried bool-to-string static borrow in ArgType<Cow<str>> to avoid tiny allocation; no benefit on this workload
Reintroduced specialized upper_filter with strict-undefined correctness via custom CheckedValue ArgType, plus allocation-free u64/i64/bool uppercase paths
Control rerun on CheckedValue-based upper_filter to gauge variance; still around 27us and confirms large improvement over prior valid baseline
Optimize upper_filter hot path by deferring kwargs-type check to Object branch only, avoiding per-call is_kwargs overhead for primitive/string inputs
Further optimize upper_filter by switching CheckedValue wrapper to direct (&State, &Value) args: avoid Value cloning while preserving strict undefined checks and object-only kwargs guard
Control rerun on (&State,&Value)-based upper_filter optimization confirms stable ~25us performance band
Bypass generic Value::from_function argument marshalling for upper by registering a dedicated UpperFilterObject with direct call(args) dispatch and optimized coercion path
Control rerun on dedicated UpperFilterObject path; confirms another very strong sample (~24.2us) with current implementation
Add VM ApplyFilter fast path for built-in upper: when cached filter is UpperFilterObject, bypass generic Value::call and dispatch directly to call_upper_filter_fast
Tried caching UpperFilterObject downcast classification per local_id in VM ApplyFilter to avoid repeated checks; added branching/state overhead regressed
Tried extra VM upper fast path specialization for single-arg calls (call_upper_filter_one) to skip args slice matching; added branches regressed
Add cached small-u64 Value table for upper filter coercion (0..1023) and reuse for non-negative i64 path, avoiding repeated decimal conversion in hot numeric upper calls
Control rerun after cached small-u64 upper coercion; result remains in ~23.5us range and confirms large win
Tried arg-count-aware one-arg upper VM dispatch (call_upper_filter_one) to skip slice arity match on fixed-arity instructions; no improvement
Tune upper numeric Value cache size from 1024 to 256 entries (covers common small integers with better locality and less initialization overhead)
Control rerun for 256-entry upper numeric cache confirms stable performance around 23.35us
Tried shrinking upper numeric cache to 128 entries; misses for common mid-range integers outweighed locality gains
Tried expanding upper numeric cache to 512 entries; no measurable win versus 256-entry cache
Tried simplifying cached_u64_value to return Value directly (removing Option/unwrap_or path); no measurable gain
Add small-integer HTML output fast path in write_with_html_escaping for U64/I64/Bool using cached string formatting, bypassing generic Value display on hot numeric emits
Control rerun on numeric HTML output fast path confirms stable ~21.7us band
Tried shrinking small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 128; misses on mid-range loop indices regressed render
Tried expanding small-integer HTML output cache from 256 to 512; no measurable improvement
Add SmallStr integer-string fast path in HTML escaping writer to bypass generic escape scan for numeric strings emitted by hot upper/filter paths
Control rerun on SmallStr integer-string HTML fast path confirms stable ~21.2us performance
Tried replacing cached small-integer HTML formatting with stack-based per-call decimal conversion; conversion loops were slower than cache lookup
Tried broad SmallStr no-escape fast path (check only escapable chars) instead of integer-specific fast path; regressed on workload
Optimize upper small-integer cache representation by storing SmallStr entries directly instead of Value, reducing clone overhead on cached numeric upper conversions
Control rerun on SmallStr-backed upper numeric cache remains around ~21.2us; improvement over prior commit is minor/noisy
Tried storing small-integer HTML format cache as SmallStr instead of Box<str>; no measurable gain
Tried VM-side unchecked upper dispatch for single-arg calls with explicit strict-undefined guard to skip per-call state checks; extra branching gave no gain
Tried replacing OnceLock small-int HTML string cache with compile-time static string table; slightly regressed versus runtime cache
Tested reducing upper numeric cache limit to 200; tiny gain likely noise/overfit to current item range, so keeping 256 for generality
Tried upper_filter no-op/uppercase path cleanup: avoid duplicate scan by using to_uppercase() directly in lowercase path and Arc clone for no-op String path; result unchanged/slightly worse
Tried manual indexed loop in is_ascii_integer_str to avoid iterator/all overhead in SmallStr numeric fast path; regressed
Tried manual decimal_str_to_value construction in upper numeric conversion to bypass Value::from(&str) helper; slightly regressed
Speed up SmallStr integer fast path in HTML writer with explicit 1/2/3-digit checks before generic integer scan, targeting dominant cached upper numeric outputs
Control rerun after 1/2/3-digit SmallStr fast path; observed expected variance but still clearly below prior baseline
Tried guarding SmallStr integer fallback scan with first-byte numeric check to skip non-numeric strings; additional branching regressed
Tried replacing per-call upper filter downcast with Environment-level builtin-upper flag (updated on add/remove_filter) to avoid RTTI checks; regressed
Tried removing generic SmallStr integer fallback scan and keeping only 1/2/3-digit shortcut; reduced generality and regressed
Tried deriving Copy for SmallStr to reduce clone overhead in hot paths; no improvement and slight regression
Tried force-inlining upper_filter to reduce call overhead; regressed significantly (likely code-size/branch effects)
Tried thread-local SmallStr cache for upper numeric conversions to avoid OnceLock atomic reads; TLS access regressed
Tried compact contiguous SmallIntFmt cache (len+3-byte digits) for numeric HTML formatting to avoid Box<str> indirection; regressed
Tried per-callsite static OnceLock<Arc<str>> in context! macro keys to eliminate repeated Arc allocations without hashmap interning; slight regression
Tried table-driven needs_html_escaping byte classification instead of range+matches branch logic; slight regression
Tried replacing slice-pattern match in call_upper_filter_fast with explicit len check branch; slightly worse
Tried force-inlining Value::get_attr_fast to reduce call overhead on frequent attribute lookups; regressed
Tried embedding internal trusted flag in SmallStr to bypass integer checks in HTML writer for cached upper numeric values; added bit-masking overhead regressed
Tried reordering upper_filter match arms to prioritize numeric variants (dominant in workload); regressed
Tried per-eval cached upper fast-path classification (compute downcast once and reuse) to avoid repeated RTTI checks; regressed significantly
Tried replacing cached_u64 unwrap_or_else closures with explicit if-let branches in upper_filter numeric arms; regressed
Tried precomputing builtin upper filter pointer once per eval and using pointer equality in ApplyFilter to avoid repeated downcasts; regressed
Tried range-pattern matches for 1/2/3-digit SmallStr detection in HTML fast path instead of is_ascii_digit calls; regressed
Fix stale String conversion path for 16-byte layout: make From<String> reuse owned allocation (Arc<String>) for non-small strings instead of re-copying via &str
Control rerun after From<String> allocation-reuse fix; confirms robust improvement and expected variance in ~20.7-20.9us band
Tried SmallStr fast path in From<Arc<str>> conversion to avoid short-string allocation copies; branch/extra work regressed
Tried String ArgType specialized string/smallstr extraction (and kwargs check for owned path) to avoid generic Display conversion; regressed
Tried routing upper_filter string conversions through From<String> (after ownership-reuse fix) instead of From<&str>; slight regression vs current best
Tried context! macro fast arms for bool literals and nested context! invocations to bypass serde-based from_serialize in common cases; regressed
Tried context! macro literal fast arm (Value::from for literals, fallback to from_serialize for exprs) to bypass serde conversion on static scalars; slight regression
Tried forcing inline on Value::from_object and Value::from_dyn_object constructors to cut call overhead in object-heavy paths; regressed
Tried split OnceLock fast path in small_u64_format (check CACHE.get() before get_or_init) to reduce hot atomic/init overhead in numeric HTML output; regressed
Replaced closure-based mark_internal_serialization guard with a dedicated drop guard struct to reduce from_serialize overhead; effectively neutral/slightly slower within noise
Tried loop object attr lookup split: avoid precomputing len for hot loop.index/index0/first/depth arms and compute length-derived values lazily; regressed
Tried context! macro specialization for nested context! expressions to bypass Value serialize/handle roundtrip in from_serialize; near-neutral but slightly slower
Tried switching internal Value serialization handle registry from BTreeMap to HashMap to speed Value roundtrip handles in from_serialize; regressed
Precompute strict/semi-strict undefined mode once per VM eval and simplify Emit default-formatter guard to a single boolean + value match, reducing hot-path branching
Control rerun after strict_undefined precompute change; observed significant variance/outlier vs keep run, need further validation before trusting magnitude
Second control rerun for strict_undefined precompute change; landed in ~20.8us band, suggesting only a small/possibly noisy benefit
Tried per-local filter cache metadata (loaded_filters_upper_fast) and manual ApplyFilter cache path to avoid repeated upper downcasts/name checks; regressed heavily
Tried caching empty safe string in Value::from_safe_string via OnceLock to avoid repeated Arc allocations for empty captures; regressed
Tried replacing small StrMap closure-based find_map lookups with manual loops in get_attr/get_attr_fast/get_item_opt; regressed
Tried VM-level loop.index specialization via Loop downcast in GetAttr plus direct Loop::get_value_by_str_fast helper to bypass dyn dispatch; severe regression
Tried threading precomputed strict_undefined bool into upper fast dispatch (call_upper_filter_fast_with_mode) to avoid per-call state undefined lookup/assert path; regressed
Tried precomputing env default-formatter flag once per eval and reusing in Emit instead of self.env.is_default_formatter() call; regressed badly
Control benchmark on current 76d44fb head to re-establish noise band before next context-conversion experiments
Retried context! nested-context specialization to skip Value serialize-handle roundtrip; still regressed versus current noise baseline
Tried context! vec! literal specialization: convert each element via from_serialize and collect into Value sequence, bypassing serde Vec container path; regressed
Tried retuning StrMap small-map linear-scan threshold from <=12 to <=2 (favor BTreeMap get for 3-key maps) in attr/item lookups; regressed
Tried manual loop only in Value::get_attr_fast StrMap small-map path (leave other lookup paths unchanged); improved over recent noise runs but not better than current best
Tried Stack::get_call_args fast return for single-argument calls (Option<u16>::Some(1)) to reduce slicing setup overhead in hot filter calls; regressed
Tried fixed-size array cache for upper small-u64 conversions (OnceLock<Box<[SmallStr;256]>>) instead of Vec<SmallStr>; regressed
Tried simplifying get_or_lookup_local to direct indexed slot check/store (drop vec.get nested Option match); regressed
Retuned Locals small-vector preallocation from 32 to 16 to reduce per-frame footprint and potential cache pressure; not better than current best
Retuned Locals preallocation from 32 to 24 to trade fewer unused slots for fewer reallocations; still slower than current best
Retuned VM value stack preallocation from 24 to 20 to reduce footprint; regressed
Retuned VM value stack preallocation from 24 to 28 to reduce growth risk in deeper eval paths; still not better than current best
A/B check: temporarily reverted 76d44fb Emit strict_undefined precompute (restored 9f876f7 vm/mod) to validate keep; measured slower than kept head
Tried replacing undefined_behavior.is_true calls in Not/JumpIf* opcodes with strict-mode precomputed checks + direct Value::is_true to cut helper dispatch; regressed heavily
Prototyped Arc<Box<str>> backing for ValueRepr::String (plus constructor/accessor updates) to test thinner shared-string payload for 16-byte mode; regressed render
Tweaked Emit strict-undefined guard to nested branch form (if strict then check variant) to improve branch prediction; still slower than current best
Tried rewriting U64/nonnegative-I64 HTML emit fast paths from match-return to if-let early returns in write_with_html_escaping; regressed
Tried replacing thread-local Value handle registry (from_serialize roundtrip) from BTreeMap to Vec with LIFO fast-path + fallback rposition/swap_remove; regressed
Tried replacing SmallStr conversion unwrap_or_else chains with explicit if-let branches in From<&str>/From<String> for Value; regressed
Tried caching current auto-escape mode in local eval variable (sync on push/pop) to avoid repeated Cell::get in Emit/EndCapture/PopLoopFrame; regressed
Tried reverse-order scans in Vec-backed Locals get/insert (iterate from newest bindings first) to reduce average lookup/update distance; regressed
Tried switching small_u64_format cache to OnceLock<[&'static str;256]> via leaked boxed strings (avoid Box<str> deref in hot path); regressed
Tried force-inlining Value::get_attr_fast and Value::get_item_opt (hot VM lookup helpers) to reduce call overhead; regressed significantly
Control run on unchanged 76d44fb head; observed lower outlier (20.60us), reinforcing benchmark variance and need for multi-sample validation
Tried GetAttr runtime fast path for loop.index using current_loop object pointer equality and direct idx load (skip dyn object attr dispatch); regressed significantly
Tried Stack::drop_top fast path for n==1 (pop instead of truncate) to speed common single-arg filter call cleanup; regressed
Tried relaxing call_upper_filter_fast inlining from #[inline(always)] to #[inline] to reduce VM hot-path code size; near-neutral but not better than current best
Tried relaxing cached_u64_value inlining from #[inline(always)] to #[inline]; single run looked faster but within observed no-change noise/outlier band, so discarded to avoid overfitting
Multi-sample validation run (7 benchmark samples, sorted median) on unchanged head to quantify noise; median around 20.71us confirms broad variance and cautions against tiny single-run gains
